data.title: DeFi Technologies financial report: holding about 208 BTC, 121 ETH and 14,375 SOL
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News DeFi Technologies announced its financial data for the first quarter ending March 31, 2025, which disclosed that thanks to the rise in digital asset prices, the asset management scale of its digital asset ETP issuing company Valour has risen to 921 million Canadian dollars (about 640 million US dollars). In addition to the adjustment of altcoin holdings, the main digital asset holdings include: 
208.8 BTC (19,459,046 US dollars), 121 ETH (213,761 US dollars), 14,375 SOL (2,156,573 US dollars), 433.322 AVAX (9,701,160 US dollars), 1,642,703 CORE (1,199,885 US dollars). (PRNewswire)

data.title: Tether's USDT Issuance on Tron Surpasses Ethereum in 2025
data.description: According to PANews, blockchain monitoring service Lookonchain reports that in 2025, Tether minted 16 billion USDT on the Tron network. This issuance brings the total USDT supply on Tron to 75.7 billion, surpassing the amount issued on Ethereum.

data.title: Panama and El Salvador Explore Renewable Energy for Bitcoin Mining
data.description: According to Odaily, Mizrachi shared insights on social media platform X following discussions with El Salvador's Bitcoin advisor Max Keiser and Bitcoin Office head Stacy Herbert. The trio explored how Panama and El Salvador could leverage renewable energy to advance Bitcoin mining. Additionally, Panama City plans to incorporate El Salvador's financial literacy textbook, 'What is Money?', into its online library system.

S. President Donald Trump, invested nearly $3 million in the token. The move brought renewed attention to EOS amid broader market consolidation, raising questions about the sustainability of the surge

data.title: Panama City Mayor Hints at Building Bitcoin Reserve
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Mizrachi posted the information of "Bitcoin Reserve" on the social platform X after meeting with Max Keiser, a Bitcoin consultant in El Salvador, and Stacy Herbert, the head of the Bitcoin Office. It is reported that the three discussed how Panama and El Salvador can use renewable energy to promote the development of Bitcoin mining. In addition, Panama City will also include El Salvador's "What is Currency?" Financial Knowledge Textbook into its online library system. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: Panama City mayor teases Bitcoin reserve after meeting El Salvador's Bitcoin leaders
data.description:
Panama City Mayor Mayer Mizrachi has hinted at establishing a city-level Bitcoin reserve in a cryptic post following his meeting with two of El Salvador’s Bitcoin policy leaders. “Bitcoin Reserve,” Mizrachi wrote to X on May 16 after meeting El Salvador-based Bitcoiners Max Keiser and Stacy Herbert. While Mizrachi didn’t share details about his discussions with Keiser and Herbert, the timing of the post came 11 days before the Bitcoin 2025 conference in Las Vegas, where Mizrachi is scheduled to speak
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/panama-city-mayor-teases-bitcoin-reserve-after-meeting-el-salvadors-bitcoin-leaders?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound

data.title: Circle Reports Decrease in USDC Circulation Amid Recent Activity
data.description:
According to PANews, official data reveals that Circle has experienced a decrease in the circulation of its USDC stablecoin over the past week. Between May 8 and May 15, Circle issued approximately 29 billion USDC while redeeming around 37 billion USDC, resulting in a net reduction of about 8 billion USDC in circulation.

The total circulation of USDC now stands at 605 billion coins. Circle's reserves are reported to be approximately $607 billion, with $73 billion held in cash and $534 billion managed within the Circle Reserve Fund. This recent activity reflects ongoing adjustments in the stablecoin's market presence and reserve management.

India is the world’s fourth-largest economy and a key buyer of many U. S. farm and tech goods. In an interview with Fox News host Bret Baier […]

data.title: Vitalik Buterin to Discuss Ethereum Consensus Layer Upgrade at ETH Beijing Hackathon
data.description: According to BlockBeats, Ethereum founder Vitalik Buterin is set to remotely participate in the ETH Beijing Hackathon, where he will deliver a speech on the topic of 'Ethereum Consensus Layer Upgrade (Beam Chain).' The presentation is scheduled for May 17, 2025, at 2 PM UTC+8.
